title: pittosporum perglabratum
intro: Pittosporum perglabratum, also known as the rough-leaved or hairy leafed totara, is a plant species native to New Zealand and is a member of the Pittosporum genus, which comprises trees and shrubs. The leaves and bark of the Pittosporum perglabratum plant have been used in traditional Maori medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, treating wounds, and reducing fever, due to its antiseptic properties. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant's leaves and bark are often prepared as teas, infusions, or poultices for their medicinal uses.
